close_reason: Implemented: RelationType enum, Relationship dataclass, relationships table + indexes, CRUD (create/get/delete), migration from related_to + questions, updated is_blocked + delete_seed cascade, 23 new tests, all 204 tests passing
description:
Build the foundation for typed relationships without touching CLI or export.

Models (src/seeds/models.py):
- Add RelationType enum (relates-to, questions, answers)
- Add Relationship dataclass (source_id, target_id, rel_type, created_at)
- Remove Question dataclass and QuestionStatus enum
- Remove related_to from Seed dataclass

DB (src/seeds/db.py):
- Add relationships table to schema, remove questions table and related_to column
- Add migration: create relationships table, populate from related_to JSON + questions table, drop old structures
- Add CRUD: create_relationship(), get_relationships(), delete_relationship()
- Add get_questions_for_seed() returning Seed objects via relationship query
- Update delete_seed() to cascade-delete relationships
- Update is_blocked(): blocked if unresolved children OR unresolved question-seeds
- Remove all Question CRUD methods
- Simplify FTS: remove question_texts column, drop question triggers, rebuild index

Tests:
- New TestRelationships class in test_db.py
- Rewrite question tests as relationship tests
- Migration test: pre-populated v1 DB produces correct relationships

See plans/question-migration.md for full context.
title: Typed relationships: models + DB layer (Phase 1)

close_reason: Pre-commit hooks configured with ruff lint, ruff format, pytest, and beads integration. All pass.
description: Install pre-commit hooks running: ruff lint check, ruff format check, full pytest suite. CRITICAL: beads already has core.hooksPath set to .beads/hooks/. Must integrate using the pattern from CLAUDE.md: (1) unset core.hooksPath, (2) symlink beads hooks from .beads/hooks/ into .git/hooks/ for post-checkout, post-merge, prepare-commit-msg, pre-push, (3) install pre-commit (uv run pre-commit install), (4) add beads as local hook in .pre-commit-config.yaml. Check sibling projects in /home/ryan/projects/outins/ for Ryan's preferred setup. Source seed: seed-81a4.1
title: Set up pre-commit hooks (ruff + pytest + beads integration)

close_reason: Solution: Claude pastes seed content in response text when user needs to see it. Bash output comes to Claude fully even if truncated on user's screen. Added guidance to prime output for nuanced behavior: display when discussing seeds with user, don't display when reading for own context. See seed-13ba for full deliberation.
description:
When running 'seeds show <id>', the output gets truncated in the terminal. User must hit Ctrl+O to expand, but then cannot type responses while in expanded mode. This blocks the workflow of viewing a seed and giving feedback simultaneously.

Possible fixes:
- Smarter output formatting (more compact)
- Pagination for long content
- Option to limit content preview with --full flag for complete output
- Output to temp file and display path

Related seed: seed-13ba
title: Fix seeds show output truncation - content not fully visible in terminal

close_reason: Implemented: CLI (ask/answer/questions/link/show/tree/doctor/blocked all use relationships), export v2 format with v1 backward-compatible import, web templates updated, 209 tests passing
description:
Wire up the new DB layer to user-facing code.

CLI (src/seeds/cli.py):
- ask: creates seed (type=question) + questions relationship
- answer: sets seed.content = answer, status = RESOLVED
- questions: queries via relationship type
- show -q: displays question-seeds via relationships
- link: writes to relationships table, add --type option (default relates-to)
- tree: reads relationships from table
- doctor: update orphan check to use relationships

Export (src/seeds/export.py):
- Export: format_version: 2, relationships as outbound edges per seed, no embedded questions
- Import: detect format version, handle v1 and v2

Prime (src/seeds/prime.py):
- Update command descriptions

Web (src/seeds/web.py + templates):
- Read relationships from DB instead of seed.related_to
- Questions page queries question-type seeds

Tests:
- Update CLI command tests
- Add v1→v2 import test, update roundtrip tests

See plans/question-migration.md for full context.
title: Typed relationships: CLI + export + web (Phase 2)

close_reason: Implemented resolution field across model, DB, CLI, display, export, FTS, and web UI. 201 tests pass.
description:
Add a first-class 'resolution' field to capture what happened when a seed reaches a terminal state (resolved or abandoned). Currently resolve just flips status with no record of the outcome, and abandon hacks a reason into the content field.

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Model (src/seeds/models.py)
- Add `resolution: str = ""` to the Seed dataclass (after resolved_at)

### 2. DB Schema (src/seeds/db.py)
- Add `resolution TEXT DEFAULT ''` column to SCHEMA
- Add migration: ALTER TABLE seeds ADD COLUMN resolution TEXT DEFAULT '' (for existing DBs)
- Update `_row_to_seed()` to read resolution column
- Update `create_seed()` INSERT to include resolution
- Update `update_seed()` UPDATE to include resolution
- Add resolution to FTS schema and triggers so resolved outcomes are searchable
- Update `rebuild_fts()` to include resolution

### 3. CLI (src/seeds/cli.py)
- `seeds resolve <id> --resolution/-r "what happened"` — writes to resolution field
- `seeds abandon <id> --reason/-r "why"` — writes to resolution field instead of appending to content
- Both should work without the flag (resolution is optional, not required)

### 4. Display
- `seeds show`: display resolution prominently for terminal seeds, e.g.:
  ```
  seed-81a4: Ship public beta
    Status: resolved
    Resolution: Shipped to outcomesinsights/seeds on GitHub. Pre-commit, CI, Dependabot all configured.
  ```
- `seeds prime`: include resolution in AI context output for resolved seeds

### 5. Export (src/seeds/export.py)
- Include resolution field in JSONL export
- Handle resolution field in JSONL import (default to empty string if missing for backwards compat)

### 6. Tests
- Test resolve with and without --resolution flag
- Test abandon writes to resolution field (not content)
- Test show displays resolution
- Test export/import round-trips resolution
- Test FTS finds seeds by resolution text
- Test migration adds column to existing DB without data loss
title: feat: add resolution text field to seeds
